content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: Ted  
Alice  
Gina

Date: November 21, 1978

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
Dwight

Subject: Termination of Community Property Arising out of a Spouse's or Dependent's Personal Injury Damage Claim

Witkin states that damages for a personal injury claim, which are collected by a spouse during the marriage will be treated as community property with certain exceptions. Civil Code Sections 5109 and 5126 provide that damages which are collected by a spouse as a result of a personal injury claim during the marriage shall be treated as separate property only when:

1) The law suit was between the spouses, or

2) The spouses were separate or a dissolution of the marriage at the time the damages were collected.

More specifically, damages will be treated as separate property if one of the present elements exist:

a) There was a rendition of a decree of legal separation or dissolution of the marriage at the time the damages were collected;

b) Either spouse, if the injured person, is living separate from the other spouse; or

c) There existed at the time the damages were collected an interlocutory decree dissolving the marriage.

Witkin also states that any personal injury claim of a dependent child which was brought on behalf of the child by the parents will also be treated as community property between the spouses.

DED:nrw
